item.title: Pentagon begins releasing UFO files
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said in a statement that the documents "have long fueled justified speculation — and it's time the American people see it for themselves."

item.title: "48 Hours:" Case By Case: The biggest cases, explained as they unfold
item.description: The cases everyone's talking about, explained as they happen. A weekly update on the biggest criminal stories unfolding now.

item.title: Russia and Ukraine accuse each other of violating ceasefires for Victory Day
item.description: Russia and Ukraine suggested ceasefires as Victory Day approaches but now they are accusing each other of violating them. CBS News' Aidan Stretch reports.

item.title: What are today's mortgage interest rates: May 8, 2026?
item.description: Want to buy a home or refinance your current one? Here are the mortgage interest rates you'll need to know first.

item.title: Iran says U.S. "crossed the point of no return" with attacks on ports
item.description: President Trump insists the ceasefire with Iran remains in effect despite an exchange of attacks that Tehran says "crossed the point of no return."

item.title: Deputy who killed Black man at grandma's home convicted of homicide
item.description: Trial jurors said they couldn't agree on the more serious charge of murder, prompting the judge to declare a mistrial on that count.

item.title: Iran says it's reviewing latest peace proposal despite strikes in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran says it is reviewing the latest U.S. proposal to end the war. This comes as both sides traded strikes in the Strait of Hormuz. CBS News' Holly Williams and Jarred Hill report.

item.title: Employers added 115,000 jobs in April, blowing past forecasts
item.description: Hiring once again exceeded forecasts, with employers adding far more than the projected gains of 65,000.

item.title: Trump reacts to hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship as U.K. identifies new suspected case
item.description: Global officials continue to monitor potential cases of hantavirus after a deadly outbreak onboard a cruise ship. CBS News' Jarred Hill reports.

item.title: Major escalation in Strait of Hormuz as U.S. and Iran trade attacks
item.description: The U.S. and Iran traded attacks in the strait of Hormuz, but the Trump administration insists the ceasefire remains. CBS News' Aaron Navarro reports, and retired U.S. Marine Corps. Col. Ray Gerber joins with more insight.

item.title: Here's how AI can help with retirement planning, and where it struggles
item.description: As more people turn to chatbots for financial advice, experts say AI offers both pros and cons for retirement planning. Here's what to know.

item.title: What scientists will be looking for as government UFO files are released
item.description: The Pentagon's release of files might help scientists shed light on unexplained phenomena and government secrets, experts said.

item.title: Protesters demonstrate against Tennessee redistricting that could give GOP an advantage
item.description: At the Tennessee capitol in Nashville, protesters demonstrated on Thursday against legislation that would break up the state's only Democratic seat. The new map would give Republicans a better chance at winning every congressional seat in November. Ed O'Keefe reports.

item.title: Health officials track passengers tied to hantavirus-stricken cruise ship
item.description: Global health officials are working to track down passengers who departed a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship weeks ago, including multiple Americans. Meanwhile, for the first time on Thursday, President Trump addressed the response to the deadly outbreak. Tom Hanson has the latest on the cruise ship and Dr. Celine Gounder breaks down what to know about the virus.

item.title: The average cost of gas in the U.S. is $4.55 per gallon. Here's how it's impacting consumers.
item.description: The average price of gas in the U.S. is now up to $4.55 per gallon, an increase of more than 50% since the war with Iran began. CBS News business analyst Jill Schlesinger explains how the spike is impacting consumer spending.

item.title: Uproar in Tennessee after new congressional map is approved that erases majority Black district
item.description: Tennessee Republican lawmakers approved a new congressional map to carve up the state's lone Democratic represented district. CBS News' Ed O'Keefe reports.

item.title: Snack mixes recalled due to possible Salmonella contamination
item.description: Mexican street corn-inspired trail mix made by Illinois food company was sold at Target and other retailers, as well as online.

item.title: Cyberattack on learning platform Canvas impacts millions of students
item.description: The online learning platform Canvas, which is used by 30 million students around the world, was hacked Thursday in a massive cyberattack. The platform is used by thousands of schools, including major universities. Jo Ling Kent reports.

item.title: Eye Opener: Cyberattack hits learning platform used by thousands of schools
item.description: A major cyberattack shutters an online learning platform used by thousands of schools. Plus, officials track down passengers, including Americans, who left a cruise ship hit by a deadly virus. All that and all that matters in today's Eye Opener.

item.title: Principal who tackled gunman: "God's hand was on all of us"
item.description: Kirk Moore, the Oklahoma principal who tackled a gunman​, sits down with CBS News for a network exclusive interview airing Monday on "CBS Mornings."

item.title: Heroic principal says tackling gunman was "just instinct": "God's hand was on all of us"
item.description: Kirk Moore, the principal at Pauls Valley High School in Oklahoma, speaks to Matt Gutman about tackling a gunman who entered the school. Moore said his response was "just instinct."

item.title: Apparent bear attack kills missing hiker in Montana's Glacier National Park
item.description: The body of a missing hiker killed in an apparent bear attack has been found in Glacier National Park in Montana, park officials say.

item.title: Sir David Attenborough | 60 Minutes Archive
item.description: In 2020, Anderson Cooper interviewed Sir David Attenborough about his film "A Life on Our Planet." Treasured and beloved around the world, the naturalist and British broadcaster turns 100 years old today.

item.title: Bear kills woman in Japan, 2 more fatal attacks suspected
item.description: Japan confirmed the first fatal bear attack of 2026 after a record 13 deaths last year.

item.title: What we know about hantavirus cases tied to deadly cruise ship outbreak
item.description: Health officials have identified at least eight confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship.

item.title: Volcano erupts in Indonesia, killing at least 3 hikers, police say
item.description: Three hikers in an off-limits area died in an eruption of Indonesia's Mount Dukono volcano, police said.

item.title: U.S. launches "self-defense strikes" on Iran, says warships came under fire
item.description: As President Trump again voiced optimism that Iran will "make a deal" to end the war, Tehran declared itself the regulator of Strait of Hormuz shipping.

item.title: USPS proposal would allow handguns to be sent through the mail
item.description: The Justice Department argues the patchwork of state laws around guns makes it difficult to take them across state lines for lawful purposes like target shooting, hunting and self-defense.

item.title: Cyberattack shutters Canvas learning platform for schools across the U.S.
item.description: A system that thousands of schools and universities use was offline due to a cyberattack.

item.title: Accused WHCD shooter seeks to disqualify Pirro, Blanche because they attended event
item.description: Attorneys for accused White House Correspondents' Dinner shooter Cole Allen asked a judge to disqualify Jeanine Pirro and other senior Justice Department leaders from the case because they were present during the incident.
